question: My friend was driving my car and had an accident. She is 22 and isn't named on the policy. Will the damage be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, there is no cover for damage that occurs while someone under 25 is driving your vehicle unless that person is your spouse or partner, or is specifically named in the Policy Schedule. Your friend is 22, is not named on the policy, and is not your spouse or partner, so the damage is not covered.
sections: What is not covered by this policy

question: Someone hit my car in a car park and drove off before I could get any details. Can I get my excess refunded?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the excess is refunded only if the claim relates to an accident with another vehicle, the driver of your vehicle was completely free of blame, the registration number and name and address of the other driver can be provided, and the other driver acknowledges their involvement. The other driver fled the scene and cannot be identified, so the name and address condition and the acknowledgment condition are not met and the excess is not refunded.

question: My car was stolen and recovered by police two days later. The contamination test came back positive for methamphetamine. I have full cover, will the cleaning be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, methamphetamine contamination cover applies to contamination connected with theft or illegal conversion of the vehicle. Your car was stolen and the contamination arose from that theft, so the condition is met and the cleaning costs are covered.

question: My son borrowed my car last month and a contamination test came back positive for methamphetamine. I have full cover. Will the cleaning costs be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, contamination damage connected with a family member is excluded. The policy excludes contamination connected in any way with you or any member of your family. Your son is a family member, so the exclusion applies and the cleaning costs are not covered.
sections: Damage by you and certain others not covered

question: I sold my car last week and the new owner has it now. Is my insurance still active until I cancel it?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, cover ceases immediately when you sell your vehicle or your interest in it. The car is now with the new owner, so the policy has already ended.

question: I forgot to put the handbrake on and my car rolled down a slope into a wall. I only have third party fire and theft cover. Is the damage to my own car covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the intentional or reckless exclusion does not apply here as forgetting to engage the handbrake is not an intentional or reckless act. However, third party fire and theft cover only applies to fire, theft, unlawful conversion, and innocent party protection from a collision with another vehicle. Damage to your own vehicle from rolling into a wall falls outside all of these, so it is not covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: I forgot to put the handbrake on and my car rolled into another car. Will the damage to the other car be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, legal liability cover applies to accidental damage to other people's property anywhere in New Zealand and applies to all cover types. Accidental damage to another person's vehicle caused by your car rolling is covered under your legal liability.

question: Someone broke into my car overnight and took the kids' car seats. I have full cover, will the car seats be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, loss of or damage to any child car seat or restraint that you own or that is in your care is covered under full cover, up to the market value of the seat at the time of the loss. Theft of the car seats from your vehicle is covered on this basis.

question: My car was broken into and they took my laptop from the back seat. Will it be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, covered items are limited to the vehicle, standard tools, accessories listed in the Policy Schedule, child car seats, and trailers. A laptop falls outside this list and is not covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: I accidentally filled my diesel car with petrol and the engine is now damaged. Will the repair to the engine be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the breakdown exclusion removes cover for mechanical, electrical, and electronic failures. Misfuelling causes mechanical damage to the engine. None of the listed exceptions, fire, collision, overturning, immersion in water, flood, malicious damage, theft, illegal conversion, or natural disaster, apply here. The damage is not covered.
sections: What is not covered by this policy

question: My flatmate borrowed my car for a few weeks and was secretly running paid rides in it. He had an accident during one of the trips. Will the damage be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, vehicles used to carry fare-paying passengers, other than private not-for-profit carpooling, fall outside the definition of private purposes and are not otherwise covered for commercial use. The car was being used for paid rides at the time of the accident, so it falls outside covered use and the damage is not covered.

question: I had alloy wheels and a roof rack fitted last year and had them added to my policy schedule. If they get damaged in an accident, are they covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the vehicle includes accessories listed in the Policy Schedule. The alloy wheels and roof rack are listed there, so accidental damage to them is covered under full cover.

question: I was on holiday in France and had a crash there. Will the damage to my car and the other driver's be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, cover applies to accidental physical loss or damage and legal liability only within New Zealand. The accident occurred in France, which is outside New Zealand, so neither the vehicle damage nor the liability for the other driver's vehicle is covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: My brother was driving my car and had an accident. He is 22 and named on the policy. Will the damage be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the under-25 exclusion does not apply to a driver who is specifically named in the Policy Schedule. Your brother is named, so the damage is covered despite him being 22.
sections: What is not covered by this policy

question: I moved house six months ago and the policy still has my old address. I had an accident last week and want to claim. Will that be a problem?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, you must tell the insurer immediately about any change in circumstance that may affect its decision about your policy. Changing address is such a circumstance and was not disclosed. Failure to comply with a policy condition gives the insurer discretion to decline the claim, so the non-disclosure is a problem for the claim.

question: A tree branch came down and crushed my car parked in the driveway. I have full cover, will the repair be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, full cover applies to any accidental physical loss or damage anywhere in New Zealand. A tree branch falling on a parked car is accidental physical damage. No exclusion applies to this cause, so the repair is covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: I moved my car to my parents' place six months ago and never updated my policy. My car was broken into there last week. I have full cover. Will the claim be paid?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, you must tell the insurer immediately about any change in circumstance that may affect its decision about your policy. A change in the location where the vehicle is kept is such a circumstance and was not disclosed. The claim is not covered.

question: I was driving in heavy rain and my car got caught in floodwater. The engine is damaged. I have full cover, will the repair be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the breakdown exclusion does not apply to loss or damage resulting from immersion in water or flood. Your engine was damaged by floodwater, which is an explicitly listed exception, so the repair is covered under full cover.

What is not covered by this policy

question: My car caught fire in the driveway and the flames spread to my neighbour's fence. They want me to pay for the damage. Will my insurance cover it?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, legal liability cover applies to accidental loss of or damage to other people's property anywhere in New Zealand. The fire started in your car and spread accidentally to your neighbour's fence, so the damage to the fence is covered under your legal liability.

question: My headlights stopped working and the mechanic said one needs replacing. Will the replacement be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the breakdown exclusion removes cover for mechanical, electrical, and electronic failures. Malfunctioning headlights are an electrical failure. None of the listed exceptions apply. The replacement is not covered.

question: The valet hit a pole while parking my car. I have full cover, will the repair be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, full cover applies to any accidental physical loss or damage anywhere in New Zealand. The valet hitting a pole is accidental physical damage occurring in New Zealand, so the repair is covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: I crashed into a friend's car in my driveway and damaged both vehicles. I have full cover, will both damages be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, full cover applies to accidental physical damage to your own vehicle. Legal liability cover applies to accidental damage to other people's property. Both coverages are triggered, so the damage to your car and your friend's car are both covered.
sections: What is covered by this policy

question: My car was stolen overnight from my driveway. It came to light during the claim assessment that the windows had been left open. Will the theft be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, you must take every reasonable care to protect your vehicle. Leaving windows open overnight when the vehicle is unattended is a failure to meet this duty. The claim is not covered.

question: I drove through a flooded patch of road that was deeper than I thought. The engine and electrical system have both failed. I have full cover, will the repair be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the breakdown exclusion does not apply to loss or damage resulting from immersion in water or flood. Your engine and electrical system were both damaged by driving through floodwater, which is an explicitly listed exception, so the repairs are covered under full cover.
sections: What is covered by this policy
What is not covered by this policy

question: I lent my car to a friend and he had an accident. His licence was suspended at the time. Will the damage to the other car be covered?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, the unlicensed driver exclusion applies: your friend's licence was suspended so he was not legally allowed to drive in New Zealand. The exclusion removes cover and no exception restores it. There is no cover for the damage to the other car.
sections: What is not covered by this policy

question: I was in a multi car pile up and the police report says I'm partially at fault. Now the other drivers are coming after me for the damage to their cars. Will my policy pay?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, legal liability cover applies to accidental damage to other people's property anywhere in New Zealand. Your involvement in the pileup was accidental and you are legally liable for the portion of damage attributable to your fault, so the policy covers your liability to pay for that damage.

question: Someone reversed into my parked car and left a note with their name, address, and registration number. They've admitted their involvement but said they don't have insurance. I have third party cover, will the repair be paid?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, innocent party protection covers accidental damage to your vehicle from a collision with another vehicle under third party only cover. All three conditions are met: the driver of your vehicle was completely free of blame, you have the other driver's registration number and name and address from the note, and the other driver is uninsured and has acknowledged their involvement. The repair is paid, up to $3,000 or market value whichever is lesser.

question: I was hit by another driver who admitted fault but refused to give me their name and address before leaving. I only have third party fire and theft. Will my car be repaired under the innocent party protection?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, innocent party protection requires you to supply the registration number of the other vehicle and the name and address of the other driver, and the other driver must acknowledge their involvement. The other driver refused to provide their details, so the name and address condition is not met and the claim is not covered.

question: I got rear ended at a red light yesterday and ended up with whiplash and a dented boot. The other driver wasn't insured. I have full cover and the medical expenses option. Will the physio be paid?
model_reasoning: According to the policy, medical expenses cover applies when a claim is agreed under cover for your vehicle or legal liability. Full cover applies to the accidental damage to your vehicle from the rear-end collision, which triggers the medical expenses cover. Physiotherapy costs that cannot be claimed from another source are covered up to $500.
sections: What is covered by this policy
